name: Cranial Cruciate Ligament Rupture
description: The cranial cruciate ligament (CCL) stabilises the knee joint. Rupture — often partial then complete — is one of the most common orthopaedic injuries in dogs, leading to instability, pain, and rapid joint degeneration.
symptoms: Sitting with leg out to side
treatment_overview: Surgical stabilisation (TPLO, TTA, or lateral suture) is the gold standard. Conservative management is only suitable for small dogs under 15 kg with partial tears.
prevention_tips: Regular low-impact exercise to build muscle
Avoid sudden explosive activities in unfit dogs
